title: Resonance Journal
text: I started this journal because the materials I keep finding do not behave like normal stone or metal. There is a pattern here. I just need to learn how to read it.

title: Pale Ore
text: I found a pale, strange ore today. It almost looks split between light and shadow. I think this ore can be smelted in a furnace. I should try that and see what it becomes.

title: Purple Ore
text: This purple ore gives off a faint pull, like it wants to react with something. It does not feel stable, but it feels powerful. I should try smelting it in a furnace.

title: Voxite Ingot
text: This Voxite Ingot has balancing properties. It feels stable somehow in my hand, even though there appears to be two parts to it. White and black, order and weight. It may be useful for controlling unstable reactions.

title: Magitek Ingot
text: This Magitek Ingot has a strong arcane pull to it. It feels like stored motion, or pressure waiting for a path. Useful, but dangerous if left uncontrolled.

title: Two Materials
text: Voxite stabilizes. Magitek energizes. They feel opposite, but not incompatible. If I can put them into a controlled workspace, I may be able to make them react without destroying the materials.

title: Resonance Workbench
text:
I think I can make a Resonance Workbench. It should work as a ritual bench, a place where unstable materials can be guided instead of simply crafted.

Current recipe:
V A V
C W C
C C C

V Voxite Ingot, A Amethyst Shard, C Copper Ingot, W Crafting Table.

title: Blocks of Power and Balance
text: Compressing these ingots into full blocks makes the effect stronger. Voxite pulls the reaction into balance. Magitek pushes it outward. If I place them around the workbench in the right pattern, they may act as an arcane focus.

title: Starter Pattern
text:
I think this pattern might focus the reaction.

    V
M - B - M
    V

Voxite above and below. Magitek to either side. The workbench in the center. It feels crude, but complete enough to try.

title: Ritual Crafting
text: The workbench does not seem to want items placed inside it. It reacts to loose materials nearby. If I drop the right ingredients near the bench and use Redstone Dust as a spark, the structure may pull them in one at a time. Redstone is consumed. The result appears above the workbench.

title: First Staff
text:
A rod needs a body, a stabilizer, a source of pull, and something to tune the reaction. A crystal shard should decide the nature of the staff.

Drop Stick, Voxite Ingot, Magitek Ingot, and one shard. Spark with Redstone Dust. Amethyst makes the standard staff; agate, amber, aquamarine, ruby, and topaz tune their own variants.

title: It Worked
text: It worked. The materials vanished one by one, and the staff formed above the bench like it was being pulled out of the reaction itself. This is not normal crafting. It is controlled conversion. The staff should wake larger apparatus patterns.

title: Apparatus Pieces
text:
If the staff is a key, then I need better parts for the lock. The workbench can form apparatus pieces the same way it formed the staff.

Magitek Core: Copper Ingot, Amethyst Shard, Magitek Ingot.
Voxite Stabilizer: Voxite Ingot, Amethyst Shard.
Amethyst Focus: Amethyst Shard, Copper Ingot, Focusing Lens, Voxite Ingot.
Spark each with Redstone Dust.

title: Careful Pull
text: The workbench is careful. Even if I drop a full stack of materials nearby, it only draws what it needs for one reaction. One cycle, one output. The rest remain untouched while the active ritual keeps them from fading away.

title: Crystal Growth
text: These crystals seem to grow in stages. Small bud, medium bud, large bud, then cluster. The clusters can be harvested for shards, and the shards seem to tune resonance differently. Agate, amber, aquamarine, ruby, and topaz each pull the staff in a different direction. Amethyst remains the simplest starting focus.

title: Future Discoveries
text: There is more to this system. Larger shapes. Better focuses. Stronger reactions. I should keep recording what I learn.
